item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Rare footage of Subject 2 actually angry"

"LETS GO SUBJECT 1!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a Melty Blood finals SCP. Oh my god I love it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know why this article felt particularly flavorless to me. Maybe it's what Roget said in that the game doesn't feel fleshed out enough for me to imagine what happens in the transcript. The charity thing is a head-scratcher, but the anomaly itself wasn't intriguing/weird enough for something like a succinct Series 2 article. Maybe I'll come back to this after a time, but it's a downvote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was a lot of fun to read, with the perfect balance of explanation and mystery for what was going on. The incident log didn't add much, but the idea of a Foundation agent being a Foundation commentator is too good, and it keeps the fun tone this has going.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That ain't Falco!

The verisimilitude is off the charts. :D I love that you've got a compulsion effect that doesn't do anything too dangerous. But if there's one thing that makes this work, it's the "50% of proceeds donated to charity". I mean, these consoles show up, people ante in, 50% is distributed to the winners, and the rest? That sense of not knowing is fantastic.

The other fantastic thing is the tone, and I can see you've gotten this far with a lot of effort. You can just tell this was written by a 70 year old researcher who thinks video games are for babies. I'll point to this in future as an example of how to write pop culture skips.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah it's a dubious bit I added to give more content to the article when a few people suggested it should be longer. I wouldn't say it's padding or "tacked on", but I do feel like I took the least interesting possible route with it. I might replace it if I can come up with something more entertaining (that doesn't push the article into full comedy; that's definitely a vibe I want to avoid).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it up until the final log. The gamer-slang dialogue clashes with the tone of the rest of the article, and I don't understand the point of the log. It doesn't really tell us anything. The story would be better without it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's got a big run-on sentence, but it's otherwise good, and I'm happy to upvote now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm giving this a tentative +1. Whilst the execution was solid, I felt as though some areas dragged on longer than they should have, and it took a while for me to actually become engaged with the article. Perhaps that's just a personal thing.

Regardless, I think there's room for improvement, but you've managed to pull it off quite successfully. Most 'gaming' articles tend to fall flat from what I've seen, generally due to a lack of clinical tone. This was consistently good throughout.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Worked on this after Scantron's suggestion, just added a bit more meat to the description.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's a bit too much specificity in the game description, I feel like some character profiles or more visual description would add more than the current more technical description. I liked the transcript for having more of that flavor. The manifestation bits are quite fun and I think if you added some more imaginative description a lot of people would like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A simple yet interesting skip, I quite like it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I considered making the incident log about this, but I think that would've pushed the article too far into comedy territory for something that already started off as a Melty Blood joke.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
3 of which have occurred during Foundation meetings.

Well, there's a mental image I'll happily upvote for.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're right, I think I could add a little more meat to the game description. I'll whip something up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
50% of proceeds to go charity!

Anyways, my only qualm is that the actual flavor (and/or plot, if any) of the game isn't really touched upon outside of the title; you could cover this with a sentence or two of the gameplay description.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh good, now I have something canon to LARP when I'm at cons in SCP cosplay!

For real tho, this is a good example of short and simple skips done right. I approve.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ooh… Let's see.

I like the execution of this. My favourite bit is that it doesnt explain where the rest of the cash actually goes. You dont overexplain.

It feels like an early Series 2 SCP. Like from 2012ish. Thats a compliment, its a kind of odd bit of oldschool. Lovely.
